2021-08-01.title: Gov. Greg Abbott bars mandates for vaccinations and masks in Texas.
2021-08-01.abstract: The state’s daily average of cases has risen more than 200 percent over the past 14 days and hospitalizations are surging, but an executive order ensures that masking and vaccinations remain voluntary.

2021-08-01.title: Even the Pope Has Prayed to Venezuela’s Beloved ‘Doctor of the Poor’
2021-08-01.abstract: In a deeply polarized country, suffering an extreme health crisis, one of the few uniting beliefs is the admiration across the political spectrum for a doctor recently beatified by the Vatican.

2021-08-01.title: With Undersea Robots, an Air Force Navigator Lost Since 1967 Is Found
2021-08-01.abstract: A recovery mission off Vietnam’s coast showed how advances in technology have given new reach to the Pentagon’s search for American war dead.

2021-08-01.title: Top Myanmar General Says Military Rule Will Continue Into 2023
2021-08-01.abstract: Six months after seizing power, the junta’s leader on Sunday extended a state of emergency for two more years. Protesters said they would persevere.

2021-08-01.title: What We Lost That Day
2021-08-01.abstract: Personal reflections from victims of the Aug. 4, 2020, explosion at the Beirut port.

2021-08-02.title: Where a Vast Global Vaccination Program Went Wrong
2021-08-02.abstract: After months of struggle, the U.N.-backed Covax alliance will soon have many more doses, promising relief for vaccine shortages in poorer countries. But it faces a deepening crisis: difficulties getting shots into arms as the Delta variant spreads.

2021-08-02.title: He Sang 2 Songs at an Election Rally. Hong Kong Says He Violated the Law.
2021-08-02.abstract: Anticorruption officials arrested Anthony Wong Yiu-ming, a Cantopop singer, who has publicly supported the Chinese territory’s pro-democracy opposition.

2021-08-02.title: In N.Y.C., a push to bring back tourists includes razing homeless encampments.
2021-08-02.abstract: As New York City strives to lure back tourists and office workers, it has undertaken an aggressive campaign to push homeless people off the streets of Manhattan.

2021-08-02.title: Covax, the vast global vaccination program, struggles to get shots into arms.
2021-08-02.abstract: Deaths from Covid-19 were surging across Africa in June when 100,000 doses of the Pfizer-BioNTech vaccine arrived in Chad. The delivery seemed proof that Covax, the United Nations-backed program to immunize the world, could get the most desirable vaccines to the least developed countries.

2021-08-02.title: Queen Guitarist’s Flooded London Basement Foretells a Climate Under Pressure
2021-08-02.abstract: After a heat wave and two Southeast Asia-style rainstorms that flooded the capital’s streets, subways and Brian May’s cellar, it seems timely to ask if the city is prepared to deal with wild weather.

2021-08-03.title: The Navy is weighing a quarantine for vaccinated visitors to Guantánamo Bay.
2021-08-03.abstract: About 6,000 people live at the base and more than a third of the adults there have declined vaccination, according to base health officials.

2021-08-03.title: Karate’s Big Question in Its Olympic Debut: Is It a Sport or a Martial Art?
2021-08-03.abstract: To Japan’s traditionalists, karate represents qualities fundamental to the national character. But to be a modern sport, the discipline has been forced to adapt.

2021-08-03.title: Outside the Olympic Cocoon, a Tokyo Abuzz Only With Cicadas
2021-08-03.abstract: The sequestered Games have left the host city feeling like anything but.

2021-08-03.title: Bangladesh is struggling to contain its worst wave of infections yet.
2021-08-03.abstract: With Bangladesh under a nationwide lockdown to fight a third wave of the coronavirus, hospitals in the capital, Dhaka, are reaching capacity and demand for oxygen is exceeding supply.

2021-08-03.title: ‘We’re Living in Hell’: Inside Mexico’s Most Terrified City
2021-08-03.abstract: Fresnillo feels to residents overrun by violence and paralyzed by fear, a testament to the failure of Mexico’s government to tackle organized crime.

2021-08-04.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows New to Netflix, Amazon and Stan in Australia in August
2021-08-04.abstract: Our picks for August, including ‘The Chair,’ ‘The L Word’ and ‘Annette’

2021-08-04.title: Assassination Mastermind May Still Be at Large, Haiti’s Caretaker Leader Says
2021-08-04.abstract: Prime Minister Ariel Henry says he believes that none of the more than 40 people detained in the killing of President Jovenel Moïse have the capacity to organize the complex plot.

2021-08-04.title: After Months as a Covid Success Story, China Tries to Tame Delta
2021-08-04.abstract: The government has imposed lockdowns and is testing and tracing aggressively to fight a new outbreak. Experts say it is time for the country to rethink its approach to the virus.

2021-08-04.title: Most children recover quickly from Covid, but some have lingering symptoms, a study says.
2021-08-04.abstract: Most children with Covid-19 recover within a week, but a small percentage experience long-term symptoms, according to a new study of more than 1,700 British children. The researchers found that 4.4 percent of children had symptoms that last four weeks or longer, while 1.8 percent have symptoms that last for eight weeks or longer.

2021-08-04.title: The F.D.A. could grant full approval to Pfizer’s vaccine by early September.
2021-08-04.abstract: With a surge of coronavirus infections ripping through much of the United States, the Food and Drug Administration has accelerated its timetable to fully approve the Pfizer-BioNTech coronavirus vaccine, aiming to complete the process by the start of next month, people familiar with the effort said.

2021-08-05.title: Is Iran’s Supreme Leader Truly Supreme? Yes, but President Is No Mere Figurehead
2021-08-05.abstract: Iran’s government is neither the democracy it claims nor the monolithic theocracy portrayed by critics. The new president, inaugurated Thursday, has real powers but is subservient to an “ultimate authority.”

2021-08-05.title: The Australian state of Victoria goes back into lockdown.
2021-08-05.abstract: Just a week after emerging from its fifth coronavirus lockdown, the Australian state of Victoria reimposed restrictions on Thursday as officials tried to avert a prolonged outbreak of the Delta variant like the one seen in the country’s largest city, Sydney.

2021-08-05.title: Second Best in the World, but Still Saying Sorry
2021-08-05.abstract: At the Tokyo Olympics, Japanese athletes who fell short of gold have apologized profusely — sometimes, even after winning silver.

2021-08-05.title: Europe is winding down its pandemic aid programs. Is it too soon?
2021-08-05.abstract: Governments want vaccinations and a business rebound to carry the economy now, but cutting aid too quickly could create economic aftershocks.

2021-08-05.title: As Italian vaccine passports come into force, tiny San Marino is left isolated.
2021-08-05.abstract: A microstate surrounded by Italy, San Marino turned to Russia and its Sputnik vaccine for protection. But European regulators have not approved the jab, making it difficult for recipients to take part in social life.

2021-08-06.title: No Work, No Food: Pandemic Deepens Global Hunger
2021-08-06.abstract: Relentless waves of the virus, combined with crises caused by conflict and climate change, have left tens of millions of people around the world on the brink of famine.

2021-08-06.title: In Qatar, the Olympic Team (Like Much Else) Is Mostly Imported
2021-08-06.abstract: The small Gulf country, most of whose residents are foreign workers, has looked beyond its borders for athletes, too.

2021-08-06.title: Reimagining Our Relationship With Nature Through Art
2021-08-06.abstract: One Australian artist gives us a glimpse into a kinder, more caring world.

2021-08-06.title: New Jersey’s governor orders schools to use masks.
2021-08-06.abstract: The furious spread of the Delta variant has prompted mask mandates and fights between state and local authorities.

2021-08-06.title: Australian Man to Be Charged in Parliament House Rape Case
2021-08-06.abstract: Brittany Higgins had sent shock waves through the country when she said she was attacked by a co-worker in 2019.

2021-08-07.title: Conservative Fellow Travelers: Tucker Carlson Drops In On Viktor Orban
2021-08-07.abstract: This week, the talk show host has been in Hungary, where Prime Minister Viktor Orban hopes to set up an ideological center for a global conservative movement.

2021-08-07.title: Is a Big Casino Footprint in a Tiny Principality a Good or a Bad Bet?
2021-08-07.abstract: Liechtenstein, where gambling had largely been illegal until 2010, now has more casinos per capita than Macau or Monaco. Some critics worry that it’s more than the principality can handle.

2021-08-07.title: They Wait Hours to Withdraw Cash, but Most A.T.M.s Are Empty
2021-08-07.abstract: Myanmar has been crippled by a cash shortage since the military seized power six months ago, plunging the Southeast Asian nation into a financial crisis.

2021-08-07.title: Fragrance Maker Dares to Sniff ‘What Life Really Smells Like’
2021-08-07.abstract: Crafting scents that demand a brave nose, and leading smelling tours through a landscape in Catalonia painted by Dalí, an evangelist of odor urges people to catch a whiff of the “sublime.”

2021-08-07.title: Taliban Take Second Afghan City in Two Days
2021-08-07.abstract: Sheberghan, in Jowzjan Province, collapsed less than 24 hours after a provincial capital in southwestern Afghanistan had fallen to the Taliban.

2021-08-08.title: The Taliban fly their flag in Kunduz as exhausted Afghan troops regroup.
2021-08-08.abstract: The rapid fall of important Afghan cities comes as insurgent fighters have pressed their offensive all around the country, dividing Afghan forces as U.S. troops depart.

2021-08-08.title: How an Obscure Swim Club Got a Say in Choosing Hong Kong’s Leader
2021-08-08.abstract: Beijing is making it nearly impossible for the pro-democracy camp to win city elections. One tool: “grass roots” groups loyal to the government.

2021-08-08.title: Fires Continue to Ravage Greece Amid Europe’s Protracted Heat Wave
2021-08-08.abstract: Multiple countries are sending firefighting aid to Greece as blazes rage for a sixth day.

2021-08-08.title: Another provincial capital, Taliqan, falls to the insurgents on Sunday.
2021-08-08.abstract: The Taliban seize a third regional capital in just one day, tightening their hold on a vast swath of northern Afghanistan.

2021-08-08.title: With More Freedom, Young Women in Albania Shun Tradition of ‘Sworn Virgins’
2021-08-08.abstract: A centuries-old tradition in which women declared themselves men so they could enjoy male privilege is dying out as young women have more options available to them to live their own lives.

2021-08-09.title: Tokyo’s Olympic Bubble? Wait Till You See Beijing’s.
2021-08-09.abstract: For the Winter Games, just six months away, China plans far more elaborate Covid-19 precautions than were taken at the Summer Olympics.

2021-08-09.title: ‘There Is a Right and Wrong Way to Tickle a Rat’
2021-08-09.abstract: Researchers in Australia tickled the rodents every day for a month to see if it would improve their emotional well-being.

2021-08-09.title: Greece works to contain wildfires, as help arrives from other nations.
2021-08-09.abstract: Wildfires were still burning in Turkey and other parts of Europe, where a protracted heat wave has created tinderbox conditions.

2021-08-09.title: The Taliban take a sixth provincial capital as government forces reel.
2021-08-09.abstract: Reeling on the battlefield, Afghan government officials face the difficult question of whether to let captured cities go in order to hold what they still have.

2021-08-09.title: Fires in Evia, Greece, Force Thousands of Evacuations
2021-08-09.abstract: The fires that have been raging in Greece for nearly a week show no signs of stopping. The biggest fire is in Evia, where thousands have already been evacuated. Many more are planning to evacuate by ferry in the coming days.

2021-08-10.title: Chinese Court Rejects Canadian’s Appeal of Death Sentence for Drug Trafficking
2021-08-10.abstract: Robert Lloyd Schellenberg initially received 15 years in prison for methamphetamine trafficking. But he was handed a death sentence in a one-day retrial in 2019.

2021-08-10.title: Your Tuesday Briefing
2021-08-10.abstract: Global reaction to a major climate report.

2021-08-10.title: Europe Reopened to Americans. Why, It Asks, Hasn’t the U.S. Reciprocated?
2021-08-10.abstract: America’s borders remain closed to most European travelers during the pandemic, even those fully vaccinated. With fears of the Delta variant raging, there appears to be no end in sight.

2021-08-10.title: A Yearlong Cry for Help, Then Death After an Assault
2021-08-10.abstract: Daniela Espirito Santo died after an assault by her boyfriend, who had been arrested, then released, by police. Her case is emblematic of Britain’s failure to protect victims of domestic abuse.

2021-08-10.title: ‘We Are Here Alone’: An Afghan Translator’s Plea for Help
2021-08-10.abstract: Starting as a teenager, Romal Noori risked his life aiding the U.S. war effort in Afghanistan. Now he’s facing a dangerous new reality as the Taliban regain power.

2021-08-11.title: Wildfires Rage in Algeria
2021-08-11.abstract: Deadly wildfires burned through forested mountains in the Kabyle region of Algeria, carving a path of destruction through small, isolated communities and killing soldiers and civilians.

2021-08-11.title: China Sentences Canadian Businessman to 11 Years in Prison
2021-08-11.abstract: The prosecution of Michael Spavor has been widely seen as political retaliation by China against Canada for the detention of a Chinese technology executive.

2021-08-11.title: Could the Taliban Take Over Afghanistan? Here’s What We Know.
2021-08-11.abstract: The Taliban seized nine cities over six days in a major escalation of their military offensive. Here’s what you need to know.

2021-08-11.title: After Tearful Departure, Cheers Greet Messi in Paris
2021-08-11.abstract: The soccer great Lionel Messi, speaking at a news conference, said leaving Barcelona had been a “very hard moment” but he was excited to join his new club.

2021-08-11.title: Here’s what to know about breakthrough infections and the Delta variant.
2021-08-11.abstract: Amid new evidence that vaccinated people with breakthrough infections can carry as much coronavirus as unvaccinated people, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has urged people in high-transmission areas to wear masks in public indoor spaces, regardless of their vaccination status.

2021-08-12.title: Locked Down and Fed Up, Australians Find Their Own Ways to Speed Vaccinations
2021-08-12.abstract: A stubborn Delta outbreak has kicked a complacent country into gear, with community members working to fill gaps in the government’s sputtering vaccine rollout.

2021-08-12.title: British Columbia Battles Nearly 300 Wildfires at Once. Here’s How.
2021-08-12.abstract: The Canadian authorities are urging residents to obey evacuation orders during the worst wildfire season since a devastating one in 2018.

2021-08-12.title: A Return to Freedom, After Nearly a Year Trapped Indoors Under Lockdown
2021-08-12.abstract: “This is more fun than I’ve had in a year.” In June, a long-term-care home resident experienced the joy of the outside world after nearly a year locked indoors because of coronavirus restrictions.

2021-08-12.title: This Boy Band Is the Joy That Hong Kong Needs Right Now
2021-08-12.abstract: The popularity of the group, called Mirror, has offered the city a rare burst of unity and pleasure after years of political upheaval.

2021-08-12.title: New Zealand, After Holding Virus at Bay, Unveils Reopening Strategy
2021-08-12.abstract: From early next year, vaccinated travelers from low-risk countries can enter New Zealand without having to quarantine.

2021-08-13.title: Taliban Forces Sweep Across Afghanistan
2021-08-13.abstract: Over the past week the Taliban have captured more than a dozen of Afghanistan’s provincial capitals, positioning them well to attack the capital, Kabul, just weeks before U.S. troops are expected to end their military mission there.

2021-08-13.title: What It Will Take to Free Australia From the Bonds of Covid
2021-08-13.abstract: An emergency room doctor in Melbourne shares her perspective

2021-08-13.title: Iran’s Health System ‘Beyond Disastrous’ From Covid Surge
2021-08-13.abstract: The Delta variant of the coronavirus has overwhelmed the country, which has been reeling from the pandemic since it began, presenting a stark test for its new hard-line president.

2021-08-13.title: 12 Years, $800 Million, One Conviction: Lebanon Tribunal Winds Down Short of Answers
2021-08-13.abstract: Established in 2009 to prosecute those responsible for the assassination of former Prime Minister Rafik Hariri, the tribunal failed to show who ordered the attack or why.

2021-08-13.title: Three more major cities are under Taliban control, as the government’s forces near collapse.
2021-08-13.abstract: With Lashkar Gah and Kandahar, the Taliban now effectively control southern Afghanistan. The western city of Herat is also in their hands.

2021-08-14.title: Poverty, Disease, Customs: Why So Many Indonesian Children Die of Covid
2021-08-14.abstract: The disease kills far more children in developing countries than in rich ones, and some factors make them especially vulnerable in Indonesia.

2021-08-14.title: A Popular Comedy’s Return in France Exposes Growing Divides
2021-08-14.abstract: After 12 years, a French spy spoof movie series featuring a clueless, racist and sexist hero re-emerges in a nation where laughter has become more complicated.

2021-08-14.title: Canada promises refuge for 20,000 Afghans as nations scramble to evacuate.
2021-08-14.abstract: Several European countries have said they are withdrawing embassy workers and seeking to evacuate those who have helped them.

2021-08-14.title: Germany adds the U.S. to its ‘high risk’ list, requiring some travelers to quarantine.
2021-08-14.abstract: American tourists and other nonessential travelers will have to quarantine for 10 days, starting Sunday, if they cannot document full vaccination or immunity from a past infection.

2021-08-14.title: Australian state goes into lockdown as Sydney’s outbreak spreads.
2021-08-14.abstract: Premier Gladys Berejiklian called the case numbers in New South Wales, now at the highest daily level since the pandemic began, “extremely concerning.”

2021-08-15.title: Map: Tracking Grace’s Path
2021-08-15.abstract: A map showing the storm’s path as it heads across the Gulf of Mexico.

2021-08-15.title: Kabul falls to the Taliban as the Afghan government collapses and the president flees.
2021-08-15.abstract: The insurgents’ conquest of the country is all but complete, and prominent politicians are talking with the Taliban about a transfer of power.

2021-08-15.title: ‘Our Hands and Feet Are Tied’: Hong Kong’s Opposition Quits in Droves
2021-08-15.abstract: The landslide victory of pro-democracy politicians in local elections in 2019 was a stunning rebuke of Beijing. Now, fear of retaliation has driven them to quit.

2021-08-15.title: Evacuation from Kabul falters as chaos at airport reigns.
2021-08-15.abstract: The U.S. embassy warned citizens to not head to the airport after reports of gunfire and said the situation is “changing quickly.”

2021-08-15.title: Before the fall of Kabul, Biden defends his decision to leave Afghanistan.
2021-08-15.abstract: Mr. Biden’s statement came hours after the Taliban seized Mazar-i-Sharif but before it captured the eastern city of Jalalabad and entered Kabul on Sunday.

2021-08-16.title: Kabul’s Sudden Fall to Taliban Ends U.S. Era in Afghanistan
2021-08-16.abstract: A takeover of the entire country was all but absolute as the Afghan government collapsed and the U.S. rushed through a frenzied evacuation.

2021-08-16.title: In Zambia Election, Opposition Leader Storms to Decisive Win Over President
2021-08-16.abstract: Voters picked Hakainde Hichilema, a businessman who had lost five previous bids for the job, to take over from Edgar Lungu, who has led the southern African nation since 2015.

2021-08-16.title: The prospect of booster shots is igniting a global health debate.
2021-08-16.abstract: Citing a lack of scientific evidence, the W.H.O. argues that extra vaccine doses should be sent to poor countries instead.

2021-08-16.title: The Taliban take over the presidential palace.
2021-08-16.abstract: After a peaceful overtaking of the palace in Kabul, the insurgents said they would use the building to announce the restoration of an Islamic emirate.

2021-08-16.title: Australia’s worsening outbreak raises concerns about Aboriginal communities.
2021-08-16.abstract: With the Delta variant spreading, only 15 percent of Indigenous people over the age of 16 have been fully inoculated, well behind the national level, according to officials.

2021-08-17.title: Khmer Rouge Leader Appears in Court to Appeal Genocide Conviction
2021-08-17.abstract: The trial is seen as the final stage of the long-running and expensive tribunal centered on the atrocities carried out in Cambodia during the 1970s.

2021-08-17.title: After 7 years of failing to fix Afghanistan, Ghani made a hasty escape.
2021-08-17.abstract: Western technocrat. Would-be populist. Wartime president. Ashraf Ghani tried to inhabit many roles during his years as Afghanistan’s president.

2021-08-17.title: The Taliban Are Back. Now Will They Restrain or Support Al Qaeda?
2021-08-17.abstract: The United States invaded Afghanistan 20 years ago in response to terrorism, and many worry that Al Qaeda and other radical Islamist groups will again find safe haven there.

2021-08-17.title: New Zealand begins a three-day lockdown after a single case is reported.
2021-08-17.abstract: The country has since reported seven cases in total of the Delta variant.

2021-08-17.title: India’s Covid-19 Numbers Have Fallen. A Third Wave Still Looms.
2021-08-17.abstract: Scientists are looking for signs that another variant could emerge in Delta’s wake, but limited sampling capabilities are hindering the search.

2021-08-18.title: The Taliban’s Leaders: Worldly and ‘Inclusive’ or Ruthless Ideologues?
2021-08-18.abstract: The Taliban claim to be more tolerant now, even willing to work with women. But have they really cast off their extremist ideology or is it all a ruse?

2021-08-18.title: The U.S. says that body parts were found inside a plane that left Kabul.
2021-08-18.abstract: As the U.S. military steps up air evacuations from Kabul, Afghanistan, the Air Force has acknowledged that human body parts were found in the wheel well of an American military cargo plane that took flight amid chaos at Hamid Karzai International Airport this week.

2021-08-18.title: Biden Rattles U.K. With His Afghanistan Policy
2021-08-18.abstract: Britain was the second-largest supplier of troops to Afghanistan, and the United States’ rapid withdrawal from the country has left some embittered.

2021-08-18.title: Glimpses of Kabul under the Taliban: Fighters patrolling roads, and lines at banks.
2021-08-18.abstract: On Wednesday morning, just before 8 a.m., a line of Afghans had formed outside a bank in the Shahr-e Naw neighborhood of Kabul, the Afghan capital.

2021-08-18.title: Hong Kong Police Arrest Students Over ‘Advocating Terrorism’
2021-08-18.abstract: The student union leaders, who were detained under the national security law, had expressed sympathy for a man who stabbed a police officer before killing himself.

2021-08-19.title: Seeking Global Recognition, Taliban Take a New Approach: Making Nice
2021-08-19.abstract: The group’s head-spinning claims of moderation follow a common playbook for rebel-held governments, with mixed lessons for Afghanistan’s future.

2021-08-19.title: The U.S. and other nations step up evacuation flights.
2021-08-19.abstract: Crowds continued to camp out near Kabul’s airport on Thursday, while U.S. troops tried to quell the chaos and accelerate the evacuation flights.

2021-08-19.title: Cuba’s acclaimed health system reels as the virus surges.
2021-08-19.abstract: Cuba’s health care system, long a source of national pride, is in acute distress, particularly in distant provinces.

2021-08-19.title: Those plastic barriers probably don’t help and may even make things worse.
2021-08-19.abstract: Covid precautions have turned many parts of our world into a giant salad bar, with plastic barriers separating sales clerks from shoppers, dividing customers at nail salons and shielding students from their classmates.

2021-08-19.title: Paralympic officials in Tokyo report the first virus case in the Olympic Village.
2021-08-19.abstract: Instances of coronavirus linked to the Games come as Japan is reporting record numbers of infections.

2021-08-20.title: President Biden will speak Friday on the troubled evacuation effort.
2021-08-20.abstract: The remarks come after days of tumult in and around Hamid Karzai International Airport since the Taliban took Kabul.

2021-08-20.title: Your Friday Briefing
2021-08-20.abstract: Hiding and hunted by the Taliban.

2021-08-20.title: Struggling to Explain Australia’s Covid Reality
2021-08-20.abstract: Australia’s experience with the pandemic has been vastly different from the rest of the world.

2021-08-20.title: South Koreans Now Dislike China More Than They Dislike Japan
2021-08-20.abstract: There is growing anti-China sentiment in South Korea, particularly among young voters. Conservative politicians are eager to turn the antipathy into a presidential election issue.

2021-08-20.title: In Rare Move, Japan Grants Asylum to Soccer Player From Myanmar
2021-08-20.abstract: Ko Pyae Lyan Aung had defied the military junta’s rule at home after its coup, and had gambled he could win the right to stay in Japan.

2021-08-21.title: Athens Is Only Getting Hotter. Its New ‘Chief Heat Officer’ Hopes to Cool It Down.
2021-08-21.abstract: Eleni Myrivili has been tasked with finding ways to help the Greek capital cope with ever-hotter heat waves that are expected to be part of life for years to come.

2021-08-21.title: He Guarded Haiti’s Slain President. And He Was a Suspect in a Drug Inquiry.
2021-08-21.abstract: Officials are examining whether President Jovenel Moïse’s killing was tied to the drug trade. The man in charge of his safety was a suspect in a major trafficking case, they say.

2021-08-21.title: Vietnam War Victims Wanted Justice. They Were Given ‘30 Bags of Rice.’
2021-08-21.abstract: South Korean troops were the largest foreign contingent fighting alongside American soldiers during the Vietnam War. They have long been dogged by allegations of brutality.

2021-08-21.title: America’s Afghan War: A Defeat Foretold?
2021-08-21.abstract: Recent history suggests that it is foolish for Western powers to fight wars in other people’s lands and that the U.S. intervention was almost certainly doomed from the start.

2021-08-21.title: Donors Send Quake Aid to Haiti, but for How Long?
2021-08-21.abstract: Misuse of previous aid money, Haiti’s political convulsions, and competing humanitarian demands may signal less generosity this time.

2021-08-22.title: Jesse Jackson and his wife, Jacqueline, are hospitalized with Covid-19.
2021-08-22.abstract: Mr. Jackson, 79, was vaccinated in January.

2021-08-22.title: The World’s Last Samaritans, Straddling the Israeli-Palestinian Divide
2021-08-22.abstract: Up a mountain in the West Bank, several hundred villagers practice an ancient Israelite religion while maintaining an ambiguous national identity.

2021-08-22.title: Tony Blair, who led the U.K. into Afghanistan, criticizes the pullout.
2021-08-22.abstract: The former prime minister of Britain called the withdrawal ‘an imbecilic political slogan about ending ‘the forever wars.’

2021-08-22.title: Sardinian Village Tries to Save an Ancient Tree Scorched by Fire
2021-08-22.abstract: A team of experts, with the help of local residents and authorities, is trying to revive an ancient olive tree in the hilltop village of Cuglieri that was engulfed in a catastrophic wildfire.

2021-08-22.title: ‘My Blood Is Boiling’: War Fever Surges in Ethiopia as Its Civil War Spreads
2021-08-22.abstract: A government drive to enlist civilians in the war effort threatens to widen the conflict, forcing ethnic groups to take sides and potentially spilling into the broader region.

2021-08-23.title: Did the War in Afghanistan Have to Happen?
2021-08-23.abstract: In 2001, when the Taliban were weak and ready to surrender, the U.S. passed on a deal. Nearly 20 years later, the Taliban hold all the cards.

2021-08-23.title: Forced to leave his home, former President Hamid Karzai remains in Kabul despite the risks.
2021-08-23.abstract: He and the Taliban have long been enemies, but he has stayed in Afghanistan, hoping to work with the militants and help shape a new government.

2021-08-23.title: Taiwan begins using a homegrown vaccine after months of shortages in imports.
2021-08-23.abstract: Critics say they worry that the vaccine, which has completed Phase 2 trials, is being used before its effectiveness and safety have been proved.

2021-08-23.title: They Were Protectors in Afghanistan. Now They Need Help.
2021-08-23.abstract: Former soldiers from Nepal have long served as private security guards in war-torn Afghanistan. Amid the chaos, they worry that they can’t get home.

2021-08-23.title: A local government in Australia killed its impounded dogs over coronavirus fears.
2021-08-23.abstract: The rural Bourke Shire Council said two of the dogs had become aggressive and the person who regularly found new homes for the animals became unavailable.

2021-08-24.title: In Shift, Israel Quietly Allows Jewish Prayer on Temple Mount
2021-08-24.abstract: Jewish activists say they are exercising their right to free worship at a site holy to Jews and Muslims. But the change upsets a longstanding compromise aimed at staving off conflict.

2021-08-24.title: In Southeast Asia, Kamala Harris’s Message: You Can Count on the U.S.
2021-08-24.abstract: The vice president rebuked China and sought to fortify the image of the United States as a credible ally amid growing questions about Afghanistan.

2021-08-24.title: Biden Faces a Trade-Off: Stop Corruption, or Migration?
2021-08-24.abstract: President Biden promised to attack corruption in Central America head on, but that goal has taken a back seat to cooperating on stopping migrants from the region.

2021-08-24.title: Many Older Americans Still Aren’t Vaccinated, Making the Delta Wave Deadlier
2021-08-24.abstract: Compared with other highly vaccinated countries, large parts of the United States have many vulnerable seniors, helping spur hospitalization and death.

2021-08-24.title: International students begin to return to U.S. universities after a Covid hiatus.
2021-08-24.abstract: In May and June, American consulates issued almost as many visas for international students as during the same period in 2019.

2021-08-25.title: Biden Sticks to Afghan Deadline, Resisting Pleas to Extend Evacuation
2021-08-25.abstract: The American withdrawal coincided with a threat by the Taliban to stop Afghans from traveling to the airport, an ominous sign that the window may be slamming shut for thousands of people desperate to leave.

2021-08-25.title: A Taliban spokesman urges women to stay home because fighters haven’t been trained to respect them.
2021-08-25.abstract: “We don’t want our forces, God forbid, to harm or harass women,” a Taliban spokesman said.

2021-08-25.title: Two House members secretly flew to Kabul on an unauthorized oversight mission.
2021-08-25.abstract: Biden administration officials said the trip by Representatives Seth Moulton and Peter Meijer had wasted resources needed to help evacuate people in Afghanistan.

2021-08-25.title: Budding Resistance to the Taliban Faces Long Odds
2021-08-25.abstract: For now, the fighters have merely two assets: a narrow valley with a history of repelling invaders and the legacy of a storied mujahedeen commander.

2021-08-25.title: Chinese Citizen Who Documented Wuhan Outbreak Falls Ill in Prison Hunger Strike
2021-08-25.abstract: Zhang Zhan was sentenced to four years for videos about failures in handling the virus. She now weighs less than 90 pounds, her former lawyer says.

2021-08-26.title: U.S. Says 1,500 Americans Remain in Afghanistan as Evacuation Enters Final Days
2021-08-26.abstract: The State Department is frantically trying to track down U.S. citizens. Tens of thousands of Afghan allies will all but certainly be left behind.

2021-08-26.title: At Least 250,000 Afghans Who Worked With U.S. Haven’t Been Evacuated, Estimates Say
2021-08-26.abstract: The current pace of evacuations is far too slow to rescue Afghans who may be eligible for special U.S. visas before Americans leave, estimates suggest.

2021-08-26.title: In This Remote American Outpost, Pandemic Recovery Is a Faraway Dream
2021-08-26.abstract: Tourism-dependent Guam has done almost everything it can to restart its economy. Its Asian neighbors may have to bounce back first.

2021-08-26.title: Israel’s Spy Agency Snubbed the U.S. Can Trust Be Restored?
2021-08-26.abstract: Israel’s new prime minister, Naftali Bennett, heads to Washington promising better relations and seeking support for covert attacks on Iran’s nuclear program.

2021-08-26.title: The Real Winner of the Afghan War? It’s Not Who You Think.
2021-08-26.abstract: Pakistan, nominally a U.S. partner in the war, was the Afghan Taliban’s main patron, and sees the Taliban’s victory as its own. But now what does it do with its prize?

2021-08-27.title: New Zealand’s Homegrown Hero
2021-08-27.abstract: Can the celebrity civil servant steering New Zealand through its lockdown succeed again?

2021-08-27.title: How Rodrigo Duterte Can Stay No. 1 by Becoming No. 2
2021-08-27.abstract: The president of the Philippines says he’ll run for the vice presidency next year. Critics see a plot to avoid prosecution for the killings in his drug war.

2021-08-27.title: What Is the Islamic State Khorasan, a.k.a. ISIS-K?
2021-08-27.abstract: Founded in 2015, the Afghan branch of the Islamic State counts both U.S. forces and the Taliban as its foes.

2021-08-27.title: After one of the deadliest attacks in 20 years of war, many people are still trying to reach the airport.
2021-08-27.abstract: The reported toll of the bombing outside Kabul’s airport rose sharply on Friday, with local health officials saying that as many as 170 people were killed and at least 200 were wounded. Yet less than a day after the attack, crowds on Friday sought once again to reach the airport, their desperation to flee the Taliban blending with grief at the enormous scale of the violence.

2021-08-27.title: A baby born on an evacuation flight is named Reach, after the aircraft’s call sign.
2021-08-27.abstract: A U.S. military commander said the baby was one of three born to women who have boarded evacuation flights out of Afghanistan.

2021-08-28.title: A Doctor’s Tour de France, One ‘Medical Desert’ at a Time
2021-08-28.abstract: Newly graduated from medical school, Martial Jardel set off on a five-month road trip to help out in areas of France suffering from a shortage of physicians.

2021-08-28.title: A Journey Through Kabul on the Day of the Fall
2021-08-28.abstract: A Times correspondent who grew up in the Afghan capital returned before the Taliban’s victory, taking in the end of one era and the fearful start of another.

2021-08-28.title: Britons, Unfazed by High Covid Rates, Weigh Their ‘Price of Freedom’
2021-08-28.abstract: Britain is reporting more than 30,000 new coronavirus cases a day, but the public seems to have moved on. Experts say this could be a glimpse into the future for other countries.

2021-08-28.title: Myanmar’s Monks, Leaders of Past Protests, Are Divided Over the Coup
2021-08-28.abstract: Some senior members of the Buddhist clergy have given their blessing to the generals in power. But hundreds of lower-ranking monks have been jailed for protesting.

2021-08-28.title: One Afghan family resettling in the U.S. finds a respite.
2021-08-28.abstract: Zar Mohammad Yousafzai applied for a job with the U.S. military forces in Afghanistan 14 years ago. The money was good, and he believed in the American mission to root out extremists there and develop their homeland.

2021-08-29.title: ‘I Won’t Go 20 Years Back in Time’: Young Afghan Women Speak Out
2021-08-29.abstract: A boxer. A singer. A journalist. Three young women found success in Kabul, Afghanistan. When the Taliban took the city, their dreams and lives were shattered.

2021-08-29.title: As the Taliban Tighten Their Grip, Fears of Retribution Grow
2021-08-29.abstract: Taliban leaders have promised amnesty to Afghan officials and soldiers, but there are increasing reports of detentions, disappearances and even executions.

2021-08-29.title: For France, American Vines Still Mean Sour Grapes
2021-08-29.abstract: French authorities have tried to outlaw hardy American hybrids for 87 years. But climate change and the natural wine movement are giving renegade winemakers a lift.

2021-08-29.title: Palestinians Finally Have Vaccines. But Will People Take Them?
2021-08-29.abstract: For months, the Palestinian authorities struggled to get doses. Now they have the shots, but disinformation and conspiracy theories have led to widespread hesitancy.

2021-08-29.title: The U.S. strikes vehicle loaded with explosives in Kabul.
2021-08-29.abstract: The possibility of civilian casualties was being investigated. The strike came hours after President Biden had warned of another possible terrorist attack.

2021-08-30.title: U.S. shoots down rockets aimed at Kabul’s airport.
2021-08-30.abstract: The attacks came with just two days remaining before President Biden’s deadline to complete the withdrawal, capping 20 years of war in Afghanistan.

2021-08-30.title: Colombia’s Troubles Put a President’s Legacy on the Line
2021-08-30.abstract: In an interview, Iván Duque defended his performance amid the pandemic and social strife, which could prompt a leftward shift in elections next year.

2021-08-30.title: Can Afghanistan’s Leading Broadcaster Survive the Taliban?
2021-08-30.abstract: Tolo came to prominence with hard-hitting news, raucous reality shows and lurid Turkish soap operas. Now there are ominous signs that a violent media clampdown is underway.

2021-08-30.title: Exile or Jail: The Grim Choice Facing Russian Opposition Leaders
2021-08-30.abstract: Experts say the current exodus of journalists and dissidents is the biggest wave of political emigration in the country’s post-Soviet history.

2021-08-30.title: New South Wales, Australia’s most populous state, has a record rise in daily cases.
2021-08-30.abstract: The Australian state of New South Wales on Monday reported its highest daily number of coronavirus cases since the pandemic began, as infections driven by the Delta variant continued to surge and millions remained in lockdown.

2021-08-31.title: Inside Kabul airport, the last American soldier leaves, and the first Taliban fighters arrive.
2021-08-31.abstract: Two images marked the end of the nearly 20-year war: a U.S. soldier leaving Kabul’s airport, and Taliban fighters nearby walking in.

2021-08-31.title: Working remotely has meant huge salaries, for a lucky few.
2021-08-31.abstract: Who wins and who loses when companies can hire from anywhere?

2021-08-31.title: The police set up checkpoints outside New Zealand’s largest city.
2021-08-31.abstract: The aim is to limit travel out of Auckland, which will remain under the highest level of Covid restrictions for two weeks after rules are eased in neighboring regions.

2021-08-31.title: 70 percent of adults in the European Union have been fully vaccinated.
2021-08-31.abstract: After a fumbling start, the European Union overtook the United States in vaccinations last month.

2021-08-31.title: 6 Sentenced to Death in Bangladesh for Killing of Gay Rights Activist
2021-08-31.abstract: The activist Xulhaz Mannan, who had started a gay, lesbian, bisexual and transgender magazine, was hacked to death in a machete attack in 2016.
